mission.title: Install and Configure LocalAI
mission.description: LocalAI is an open-source alternative to proprietary AI models, designed for self-hosting on consumer-grade hardware. It supports various AI tasks such as text generation, audio, and image processing without requiring a GPU. Ideal for users seeking a local-first solution with decentralized inference capabilities.

steps.title: Install the LocalAI CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the LocalAI command-line interface, which is necessary for managing LocalAI resources.

```bash

curl -Lo localai https://github.com/go-skynet/LocalAI/releases/download/v3.12.1/localai-linux-amd64

chmod +x localai

sudo mv localai /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the LocalAI binary for Linux.
> Make the downloaded binary executable.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kubernetes cluster using Minikube, which is suitable for local development and testing.

```bash

minikube start --kubernetes-version=v1.30.0 --cpus=2 --memory=4096

```

> Start Minikube with specified resources.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the Minikube cluster.

```bash

kubectl config use-context minikube

```

> Set the current context to Minikube.

steps.title: Deploy LocalAI on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys LocalAI to your Kubernetes cluster using a Helm chart.

```bash

helm repo add localai https://charts.localai.io

helm install localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai --create-namespace

```

> Add the LocalAI Helm repository.
> Install LocalAI using Helm.

steps.title: Verify LocalAI Deployment
steps.description:
This step checks if the LocalAI pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> List all pods in the LocalAI namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LocalAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes the LocalAI deployment and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall localai --namespace localai

kubectl delete namespace localai

```

> Uninstall the LocalAI Helm release.
> Delete the LocalAI namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LocalAI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step will upgrade LocalAI to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase == "Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> Drain all running pods to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrade LocalAI to the specified version.
> Verify that the LocalAI pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n localai -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase == "Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or the repository is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure the repository is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LocalAI application may be misconfigured or missing required resources.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n localai' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server may be overloaded or unresponsive.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Kubernetes cluster and ensure it is healthy.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The image cannot be pulled due to authentication issues or incorrect image name.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and check if authentication is required for the image repository.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved API endpoints for text generation.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated older model formats in favor of the new gguf format.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from version 3.10.x, ensure to migrate your models to the gguf format.

resolution.summary: You should see the LocalAI pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n localai'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
